name: Org Governance Panel
description: Organization governance workspace for managing approval policy, reviewing pending packages, and scanning the audit trail inside a shared dashboard shell.

workspace.description: Governance control surface with an overview header, policy controls, pending approvals list, and compact audit log.

slots.workspace-header: Primary governance intro card with organization selector, status badges, and contextual CTA rail
slots.kpi-strip: Optional KPI row for approvals, private packages, seats, and recent approval actions
slots.policy-panel: Policy control panel with toggle rows and supporting copy
slots.approval-panel: Pending approval queue with item summary and approve/reject actions
slots.audit-panel: Compact audit log panel with scope/action filters and chronological entries

visual_brief: A calm but authoritative governance workspace. The top card should read like a control room rather than a generic form: organization identity chips, role/tier context, and a strong action rail to adjacent workspaces. Policy controls should feel deliberate and readable, with each rule presented as a decision row instead of raw checkbox clutter. Approval items should read like compact review cards with a clear split between package identity and decision actions. The audit area should feel dense but structured, with filters aligned into a tidy control band and event entries stacked in a subtle bordered log.

responsive.mobile: Everything stacks vertically. Header CTA rail wraps beneath context chips. Policy rows and approval cards turn into single-column cards before controls crowd. Audit filters stack.
responsive.tablet: Header and audit controls may use split rows, but decision controls still break cleanly before feeling compressed.
responsive.desktop: Workspace header can use a split layout with context on the left and org selector / CTA rail on the right. Policy, approvals, and audit remain visually compact without feeling dense.

motion.micro: Policy toggles, approval actions, and audit filter controls may animate softly over 150-200ms, but the workspace should remain composed and administrative.
motion.transitions: Approval queues and audit log filters should update with light cross-fades or row reveals instead of abrupt reflows.

layout_hints.cta_treatment: Header actions should read like strong workspace transitions, not secondary ghost text. Use icon-led CTA rails or compact button groups with one clear primary action.
layout_hints.policy_rows: Each rule should have explanatory copy on the left and the control on the right. On narrow widths, the control drops beneath the copy without losing alignment.
layout_hints.audit_filters: Filters belong in a compact action band. Avoid ad hoc select sizing and preserve consistent x-axis insets across title, filters, and log entries.

keyboard: Tab moves through policy rows, approval actions, and audit filters in operational order.
announcements: Policy changes, approval outcomes, and audit filter updates should be announced clearly.
accessibility.focus_management: When filters or decisions update the panel, keep focus on the initiating control or move it to the refreshed heading so the operator keeps context.
